cases.title: Appeal of TE Charles Tarter vs. Evangel Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Tarter appealed his deposition by Evangel Presbytery's Judicial Commission for failure to submit to presbytery authority. The SJC sustained multiple specifications of error — including that the Presbytery's committee had no authority to make certain demands, the indictment was inadequate, and hearsay evidence was improperly admitted — and remanded the case to Evangel Presbytery for constitutionally compliant process or dismissal.

cases.title: RE William G. Mitchell vs. The Presbytery of the Ascension
cases.synopsis: RE Mitchell appealed his indefinite suspension from office as Ruling Elder by the Presbytery of the Ascension, arising from allegations he broke a promise to take a sabbatical from Session and failed in his duties as Clerk of Session. The SJC vacated the judgment on Specification No. 2 (promise-breaking) and remanded for possible new trial, and reversed and dismissed Specification No. 6 (clerk duties) as insufficiently serious to warrant trial.

cases.title: Dunn and Pesnell vs. Philadelphia Metro West Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Dwight Dunn and TE Darin Pesnell

cases.synopsis: TE Dunn and TE Pesnell complained that Philadelphia Metro West Presbytery erred in adopting its commission's recommendation to institute process and proceed to trial against TE David Swavely for alleged pastoral and spiritual abuse. The SJC denied the complaint, finding that the Presbytery had sufficient authority to proceed to trial and that the errors alleged did not rise to a reversible level.

cases.title: Complaint of the Session of Hope Community Church vs. Central Carolina Presbytery
topics: session authority over time and place of worship
cases.synopsis: The Session of Hope Community Church complained that Central Carolina Presbytery erred in adopting a policy requiring a multi-site session to eventually particularize its second worship site as a distinct new church. The SJC sustained the complaint, holding that the BCO expressly vests the Session with authority over the time and place of worship and the Presbytery's mandatory particularization requirement exceeded its constitutional powers and was annulled.

cases.title: Session of First Presbyterian Church North Port vs. The Presbytery of Southwest Florida

parties.complainant_or_appellant: Session of First Presbyterian Church North Port

cases.synopsis: The Session of First Presbyterian Church North Port complained that the Presbytery of Southwest Florida erred in directing and instructing it to initiate formal process against a communing member after the session had obtained a civil trespass order against her without formal ecclesiastical charges. The SJC sustained both issues, finding that Presbytery exceeded its constitutional authority in directing the Session to act and in assuming direct contact with the member.
